docs.title: GOAL
docs.text: This short course on the Internet of Things is designed to give a first understanding of the main issues in developing IoT solutions. We do believe the most efficient way to learn this subject is experimenting, this is way the course encourage attendants to hands-on. No specific background is needed, even if a basic knowledge of electronics and computer science might help.

docs.title: DAY 1 (3 hours)
docs.text:
• Introduction to the IoT
  • IoT and Big Data: we can quantitatively measure phenomena that up to yesterday we were only able to guess
  • A network of Resource Constrained Devices: if computational power, memory, energy, bandwidth and costs are not constrained, it is likely Internet, not Internet of Things

docs.title: DAY 2 (3 hours)
docs.text:
• The reference hardware: ESP32
  • The Maker approach: focus on effectiveness, namely doing the right things
  • Hands on with the Arduino Ide and Wokwi
  • A simple integration of sensor/actuator
  • Communicating over WiFi

docs.title: DAY 3 (3 hours)
docs.text:
• The Engineer approach: beyond effectiveness, efficiency matters, namely doing the thing right
  • The LEAN approach: build, measure, learn
  • The need of an Operating System
  • RIOT and FREERTOS
  • https://docs.espressif.com/projects/esp-idf/en/latest/esp32/api-reference/system/freertos.html
  • Hands on on FREERTOS or RIOT
  • A simple integration of sensor/actuator
  • Communicating over WiFi

docs.title: DAY 4 (3 hours)
docs.text:
• The Very Large Scale IoT Testbed IoT-LAB
  • FreeRTOS on IoT-lab
  • openlab/appli/iotlab_examples/tutorial/
  • https://www.iot-lab.info/legacy/tutorials/getting-started-tutorial/index.html
  • https://iot-lab.github.io/docs/tools/consumption-monitoring/
  • A simple Project

docs.title: Tools and Materials
docs.text:
• ESP32-WROOM-32 e.g. Available on Amazon
  • Sensors and Actuators To be Selected
  • The Espressif software development environment
  • Arduino Ide
  • Wokwi
  • RIOT
  • FREERTOS
  • IoT-LAB
  • Thingsboard

docs.title: Takeaway ONE
docs.text:
• IoT is fuel for Big Data Analytics

  • "When you can measure ... you know something". Lord Kelvin docet.

  • IoT provide quantitative evidences on phenomena that we used to investigate only qualitatively

docs.title: Takeaway TWO
docs.text:
• IoT is a network of resource constrained devices
  • Constraints require design choices
  • Beyond the maker approach, namely when effectiveness meets efficiency
